pr.title: chore(deps-dev): bump dev-dependencies group with 11 updates (replaces PR #62)
pr.body:
Manually re-applies the 11 dev dependency bumps from the conflicted, non-maintainer-editable Dependabot PR #62, regenerating `pnpm-lock.yaml` cleanly from `main`.

## Dependency updates (`package.json`)

| Package | From | To |
|---|---|---|
| `@clerk/testing` | `^2.0.29` | `^2.0.30` |
| `@playwright/test` | `^1.58.2` | `^1.60.0` |
| `@tanstack/devtools-vite` | `^0.3.11` | `^0.7.0` |
| `@tanstack/eslint-config` | `^0.3.0` | `^0.4.0` |
| `@vitejs/plugin-react` | `^5.0.4` | `^5.2.0` |
| `dotenv` | `^17.2.3` | `^17.4.2` |
| `drizzle-kit` | `0.31.8` | `0.31.10` |
| `eslint` | `^9.39.2` | `^9.39.4` |
| `prettier` | `^3.5.3` | `^3.8.3` |
| `typedoc` | `^0.28.17` | `^0.28.19` |
| `web-vitals` | `^5.1.0` | `^5.2.0` |

## pnpm v10+ config migration (`pnpm-workspace.yaml`)

During lockfile regeneration, pnpm 11.2.2 emitted a hard warning that `pnpm.overrides` in `package.json` is **no longer read** — the security overrides were being silently dropped. Migrated all overrides and `allowBuilds` entries to `pnpm-workspace.yaml` (the canonical location since pnpm v10):

```yaml
# pnpm-workspace.yaml (new)
overrides:
  tar: ^7.5.8
  esbuild: ^0.25.0
  # … all existing overrides …

allowBuilds:
  '@clerk/shared': true
  '@sentry/cli': true
  esbuild: true
  unrs-resolver: true
```

The stale `pnpm.overrides` block has been removed from `package.json`, and `docs/dependency-overrides.md` updated to reflect the new config location.

> 
> Goal:

> - Preserve the intended dependency versions from the Dependabot PR.

> 
> Requested dependency updates to apply in `package.json`:
> - `@clerk/testing`: `^2.0.29` -> `^2.0.30`
> - `@playwright/test`: `^1.58.2` -> `^1.60.0`
> - `@tanstack/devtools-vite`: current base version -> `^0.7.0`

> - `@vitejs/plugin-react`: current base version -> `^5.2.0`
> - `dotenv`: current base version -> `^17.4.2`
> - `drizzle-kit`: current base version -> `0.31.10`

> - `prettier`: current base version -> `^3.8.3`
> - `typedoc`: current base version -> `^0.28.19`
> - `web-vitals`: current base version -> `^5.2.0`
> 
> Implementation guidance:
> - Start from the current `main` branch, not the Dependabot branch.
> - Inspect the current `package.json` on `main` before editing, since some versions on `main` may already differ from the original PR snapshot.
> - Update only the intended dev dependency entries unless additional minimal changes are required to make lockfile generation succeed.
> - Regenerate `pnpm-lock.yaml` using the repo’s package manager workflow.
> - If package manager metadata files must also change as part of a correct lockfile update, include those changes.
> - Do not make unrelated refactors.
> 
> Validation:
> - Run the project’s relevant install / lockfile generation / test or lint commands if available and practical.
> - Ensure the PR cleanly replaces the intent of PR #62.
> 
> In the PR description, mention that this PR replaces the conflicted Dependabot PR #62.
> 
> The following is the prior conversation context from the user's chat exploration (may be truncated):
> 
> User: resolve the merge conflicts in this pull request
> 
> Assistant: I can’t directly resolve this PR for you because the PR branch is owned by `dependabot[bot]` and GitHub reports `maintainer_can_modify: false`, so I don’t have a writable branch to update from here.
> 
> What I *can* confirm:
> - PR #62 is currently **not mergeable**.
> - Its mergeability state is **`dirty`**, which means there are merge conflicts.
> - The conflicts are in the two changed files: `package.json` and `pnpm-lock.yaml`.
> 
> The fastest fix is usually:
> 1. Go to PR #62.
> 2. Add a PR comment:
>    ```text
>    @dependabot rebase
>    ```
> 3. Wait for Dependabot to recreate/rebase the branch against `main`.
> 4. Re-check mergeability.
> 
> Because this is a Dependabot PR and the PR body explicitly says Dependabot can resolve conflicts itself, that is the safest path here.
> 
> If you want, I can also help you do the manual resolution strategy:
> - keep the dependency bumps from this PR in `package.json`
> - regenerate `pnpm-lock.yaml` from the updated `main`
> - open a replacement PR
> 
> If you want me to proceed with that, tell me to **create a replacement PR for `aaron-howard/training-certify`**.
